name: Portuguese Almond & Potato Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake or microwave potato until soft. Force the pulp through a coarse sieve or potato ricer. There should be 1 cup lightly packed. Set aside to cool.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter the sides of an 8 1/2-inch spring form pan, and line the base with a circle of baking parchment; butter the parchment.
3. Using a nut mill, grind almonds to a powder and stir in the baking powder. Set aside.
4. Beat egg whites until they start to stiffen. Sprinkle with half of the sugar, a little at a time, and continue beating until stiff and glossy.
5. Beat remaining sugar with butter into the mixture, then beat in egg yolks, brandy, and almond extract.
6. Using a large rubber spatula, fold in the potato, ground almonds, and egg whites.
7. Spoon batter into prepared pan; bake for 35 to 40 minutes until a tester comes out dry. Let cool in pan for 10 minutes.
8. Run a knife blade around the edge of the pan before releasing the sides. Place the cake on a rack and let it cool completely. The cake will sink slightly in the middle.
9. Turn the cake right side up, peel off the parchment, and place it on a serving plate. Dust with powdered sugar before serving.
10. Marzipan "potatoes": Pinch off pieces of marzipan and roll into elongated balls, each about the size of a large marble. Make these slightly irregular, like tiny new potatoes. Roll in cocoa to simulate brown potato skin. Cut several in half to show the white interior. Arrange a small group on top of the cake, and place the rest in twos and threes around the edge of the plate.
Serves 10.
Per serving: 235 calories, 5 g protein, 23 g carbohydrate, 14 g fat (5 g saturated), 76 mg cholesterol, 131 mg sodium, 2 g fiber.
